title: Check the Launch Winds
description: Measure wind speed, interpret limits, and re-test until launch conditions are safe.

dialogue.text: Nimbus here. Wind is data, not vibes. We fly only when the numbers say go.

options.text: Copy that. What's the safe range?

dialogue.text: Hard stop if sustained wind is above 8 m/s or gust spread is above 3 m/s. If either limit is exceeded, we scrub and correct before launch.

options.text: Understood. I'll collect a reading artifact now.

dialogue.text: Take two anemometer readings at launch height and log them to your wind card.

options.text: Wind card logged. Let's interpret the results.

dialogue.text: Interpretation step: PASS means sustained <= 8 m/s and gust spread <= 3 m/s. FAIL means any value above limits.

options.text: PASS: both values are inside limits.
FAIL: values are out of range. Start corrective plan.

dialogue.text: Corrective branch: delay launch, move to a more sheltered pad orientation, and wait for a calmer window. Safety stop remains active until re-test passes.

options.text: Corrections complete. Re-test required before we continue.

dialogue.text: Run a mandatory re-test. No launch clearance without a fresh in-range measurement card.
Excellent discipline. We have measurement evidence and a documented pass. Launch window is now open.
